Container.Name: bifrost
Container.Overview: Self-hosted smart-home control hub. It brings your lights (Philips Hue, Govee, LIFX), audio (Sonos, Onkyo / Integra), power devices (switches, plugs, fans), and TVs/streamers under one web UI and REST API, and can also surface any Home Assistant integration as a Bifrost device. Organize devices into rooms, save scenes (full-state snapshots you can restore in one tap), and control everything by voice or AI assistants through the embedded MCP server. It aims to keep device connections live and show each device's real state. Runs as a single binary with one SQLite file; pair Hue from the UI via the bridge link button. Runs on the host network so device auto-detect works: discovery uses SSDP/eISCP broadcast, multicast, and a local-subnet sweep, none of which cross a bridged container's NAT. The web UI is on port 3000 (set by Bind Address below); change that variable to use a different port.

Config.Name: Data Directory
Config.Description: Directory for the SQLite database (bifrost.db). All state lives here: password, providers (credentials encrypted), lights, scenes, groups, floor plans.

Config.Name: Secret Key
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Encrypts provider credentials at rest (AES-256-GCM). Generate one with: openssl rand -hex 32. Changing it later orphans stored provider credentials (you would re-add providers).

Config.Name: Database URL
Config.Description: SQLite connection string. Leave at the default unless you move the Data Directory mapping.

Config.Name: Bind Address
Config.Description: Address and port the app listens on. With host networking this is the actual host port — open http://[server-ip]:3000 after install. If you change the port here (e.g. 0.0.0.0:8080), browse to that port instead; the WebUI shortcut is fixed at 3000.

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Log filter, e.g. bifrost=info or bifrost=debug.
